name: Jimmy Carter
title: 39th President of the United States
bio: American politician and humanitarian who served as the 39th President of the United States from 1977 to 1981. After leaving office, he became one of the most active former presidents in history, founding the Carter Center and dedicating decades to Habitat for Humanity, conflict resolution, and global health.
faith: A lifelong Southern Baptist and born-again Christian whose faith was the defining force of his life. He taught Sunday school at Maranatha Baptist Church in Plains, Georgia for over 40 years — continuing well into his 90s. He once said, 'My faith demands that I do whatever I can, wherever I am, whenever I can, for as long as I can with whatever I have to try to make a difference.' His post-presidency of humble service became one of the most powerful Christian testimonies in American political history.

sources.title: Faith of Jimmy Carter — Wikipedia
sources.note: His Christian faith and Sunday school teaching

tagline: Taught Sunday school for 40 years and served the world in Jesus' name
